title: Bridge Essay: Enactive-Dynamic Social Cognition and Active Inference.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Enactive-Dynamic Social Cognition and Active Inference.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 35572328. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2022.855074. This aim of this paper is two-fold: it critically analyses and rejects accounts blending active inference as theory of mind and enactivism; and it advances an enactivist-dynamic understanding of social cognition that is compatible with active inference. While some social cognition theories seemingly take an enactive perspective on social cognition, they explain it as the attribution of mental states to other people, by assuming representational structures, in line with the classic Theory of Mind...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 3 (Mind as Computer Critique). Evaluation: Good (score=2.0)
